name: apparmor
title: apparmor
description:
user-space parser utility for AppArmor
apparmor provides the system initialization scripts needed to use the
AppArmor Mandatory Access Control system, including the AppArmor Parser
which is required to convert AppArmor text profiles into machine-readable
policies that are loaded into the kernel for use with the AppArmor Linux
Security Module.

commands.name: aa-exec
commands.description: confine a program with the specified AppArmor profile

commands.name: aa-features-abi
commands.description: Extract, validate and manipulate AppArmor feature abis

commands.name: aa-load
commands.description: load precompiled AppArmor policy from cache location(s)

commands.name: aa-status
commands.description: display various information about the current AppArmor policy.

commands.name: apparmor_parser
commands.description: loads AppArmor profiles into the kernel

commands.name: apparmor_status
commands.description: display various information about the current AppArmor policy.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use aa-enabled because user-space parser utility for AppArmor.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use aa-enabled as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `aa-enabled [options]` as documented upstream for aa-enabled.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair aa-enabled with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `aa-enabled --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
